Hydroflouric acid and aqua regia

exp_method_translation: HF/AR, anion and cation exchange chromatography
exp_method_desc: Digest in HF and aqua regia, double spike, ammonium persulfate ((NH4)2S2O8), anion exchange AG1-X8 resin, cation exchange AG50-WX8 resin
Three acid digestion with nitric acid, hydrochloric acid and hydrofluoric acid

exp_method_translation: HNO3/HCl/HF, anion exchange chromatography (AG1X8 or AGMP1 resin), sample-standard bracketing
exp_method_desc: HNO3/HCl/HF, anion exchange chromatography with AG1 X8 or AG MP-1 resin, sample-standard bracketing.

exp_method_translation: HNO3/HCl/HF, anion exchange (AG1X8 resin), cation exchange (AG50W-X8 resin)
exp_method_desc: HF/HNO3/HCl digestion, followed by two-step anion exchange AG1-X8 resin and cation exchange AG50W-X8 resin. See e.g. Barling et al. 2001, Duan et al. 2010.
Three-acid digestion with nitric acid, hydrochloric acid and hydrofluoric acid followed by aqua regia digest
HNO3/HCl/HF followed by H2O2 to remove organic residues
Digestion by HF/HNO3/HCl, ion exchange chromatography UTEVA resin
Three acid digestion with hydrofluoric acid, nitric acid and perchloric acid

exp_method_translation: HF/HNO3/HClO4, HCl/HNO3, two-step cation exchange chromatography  (AG50W-X12 resin)
exp_method_desc: HF/HNO3/HClO4 digest, conc HCl and HNO3, two column cation exchange chromatography AG 50W-X12 resin. See Pogge von Strandmann et al. 2011, James and Palmer 2000.

exp_method_translation: HF/HNO3/HClO4 digest, HCl/HNO3, three cation exchange chromatography
exp_method_desc: HF and HNO3, followed by HNO3 and HCl (+/- perchloric acid), three-column cation exchange AG 50W-X12 resin chromatography. See Moriguti and Nakamura 1998 (first three columns)

exp_method_translation: HF/HNO3/HClO4, anion exchange (AG1X8 resin), cation exchange (AG50W-X8 resin)
exp_method_desc: HF/HNO3/HCl digestion, followed by two-step anion exchange AG1-X8 resin and cation exchange AG50W-X8 resin
Three-acid digestion with nitric acid, hydroflouric acid and perchloric acid, taken up and dissolved in AR

exp_method_translation: HNO3/HF/HClO4, aqua regia, anion exchange chromatography (AGMP1 resin), sample-standard bracketing
exp_method_desc: HNO3/HF/HClO4, AR, anion exchange chromatography
Acid digestion with nitric acid, hydrofluoric acid, perchloric acid and boric acid (used to prevent formation of Al complexes).
Three acid digestion with hydrofluoric acid, nitric acid and perchloric acid, followed by ion exchange chromatography with UTEVA resin. See e.g. Weyer et al. 2008.

exp_method_translation: HF/HClO4/HNO3, TCF filter (Method D of Stüeken et al. 2013)
exp_method_desc: HF-HClO4-HNO3 digest, filtered with thiol cotton fibers (TCF), Se desorbed with HNO3 redissolved in HCl for analysis. Method D of Stüeken et al. 2013.

exp_method_translation: HF/HClO4/HNO3, TCF filter +/- Aqua regia (Average of Method D and Method E of Stüeken et al. 2013)
exp_method_desc: HF-HClO4-HNO3 digest, filtered with thiol cotton fibers (TCF), +/- treatment with Aqua Regia, Se desorbed with HNO3 and redissolved in HCl for analysis. Average of Method D and Method E of Stüeken et al. 2013.

exp_method_translation: HF/HClO4/HNO3, TCF filter, Aqua Regia (Method E of Stüeken et al. 2013)
exp_method_desc: HF-HClO4-HNO3 digest, filtered with thiol cotton fibers (TCF), treated with Aqua Regia, Se desorbed with HNO3 partially evaporated and redissolved in HCl for analysis. Method E of Stüeken et al. 2013.
NOT USED Acid digestion in HNO3 and HF, Aqua Regia
Four acid digestion with nitric acid, perchloric acid, hydrofluoric acid and hydrochloric acid. Common commercial lab method see e.g. MA200, MA250, Ultratrace 4
Four-acid digest, details unknown
Four acid digestion with hydrofluoric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid and perchloric acid and ion exchange separation
Four acid digestion with hydrofluoric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid and perchloric acid, ion exchange separation with TRU resin for uranium isotopes. See Dang et al. 2016, 2018.
NOT USED Digestion in HF and HNO3, followed by digestion in aqua regia, and HCl
Five acid digestion with nitric acid, perchloric acid, hydrofluoric acid, boric acid and hydrochloric acid
Multi-acid digestion with hydrofluoric acid (originally a CMIBS method)
Microwave digest followed by three acid digestion with nitric acid, hydrochloric acid and hydrofluoric acid
Microwave digest followed by three acid digestion with nitric acid, hydrochloric acid and hydrofluoric acid, followed by addition of H2O2 to remove organics
Microwave assisted HF-based 4-acid digest (originally a Labwest method)

exp_method_translation: Methanol/ultrasonic cleaning, two-stage cation exchange chromatography (AG50W-X12 resin)
exp_method_desc: Methanol (+/-ultrasonic) followed by two column cation exchange chromatography AG 50W-X12 resin (HCl as eluent). See Pogge von Strandmann et al. 2011, James and Palmer 2000.

exp_method_translation: Methanol, 0.2M ammonium acetate, 0.5M acetic acid, cation exchange (Sr Spec resin)
exp_method_desc: Methanol, 0.2 M ammonium acetate, 0.5 M acetic acid at room temperature for 2 h to dissolve the calcite fraction, cation exchange chromatography with Eichrom Sr Spec resin

exp_method_translation: 0.2M ammonium acetate, 0.5M acetic acid, cation exchange (Sr Spec resin)
exp_method_desc: 0.2 M ammonium acetate (pH approx. 8.2), 0.5M acetic acid, cation exchange  with Eichrom Sr Spec resin
Digestion using 1 M ammonium acetate solution
Digestion using 1 M ammonium acetate solution, two step ion exchange chromatography using RE Resin (Triskem)

exp_method_translation: 1M ammonium acetate, 0.05N HCl in 4hr/2hr/10min seq. steps, cation exchange (AG50W-X12 resin)
exp_method_desc: leached in 1M ammonium acetate, dissolved in three steps (4h, 2h and 10 minutes) into a 0.05N hydrochloric acid solution [..] redissolved in 6N HCl, cation exchange chromatography AG50W-X12 resin
blahblah
Fish debris extraction sensu Schenau & De Lange, 2000. 2 M NH4Cl multiple times.
Digestion with NH4HF2 according to the method of Zhang et al. 2012

exp_method_translation: Ammonium metavanadate/HF acid digestion with K2CrO4
exp_method_desc: Ammonium metavanadate/HF acid digestion with K2CrO4

exp_method_translation: Sodium carbonate and zinc oxide sinter digestion. (CMIBS method)
exp_method_desc: Sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) and zinc oxide (ZnO) sinter digestion (originally a CMIBS method).
Sodium peroxide sinter/fusion

exp_method_translation: NaCl (repeated leaches), HCl, BaCl2 (Wotte et al. 2012)
exp_method_desc: Repeated NaCl leaching, HCl, precipitation of BaSO4 with BaCl2. Method of Wotte et al. 2012.

Treated with NaCl and NaOCl, HCl
Treated with NaCl and NaOCl, HCl, BaCl2 to precipitate BaSO4.
1M sodium acetate, pH 4 with acetic acid

exp_method_translation: 1M sodium acetate (AcOH buffered), two stage cation exchange chromatography  (AG50W-X12 resin)
exp_method_desc: weak leach of 1 M Na acetate buffered to pH 5 with acetic acid, two-column cation exchange chromatography AG 50W-X12 resin HCl as an eluent. See Pogge von Strandmann 2011, James and Palmer 2000.

exp_method_translation: 1M sodium acetate, ion exchange chromatography (UTEVA resin)
exp_method_desc: Selective digest in 1M sodium acetate buffer solution, ion exchange chromatography with UTEVA resin

exp_method_translation: Sum of extract first with sodium acetate, followed by HF/HNO3/HClO4 digestion
exp_method_desc: Sequential extraction modified SeqA acetate extraction followed by multi-acid (nitric/hydrofluoric/perchloric) digestion. See Adeboye et al. 2020.

exp_method_translation: NaOCl(bleach), HCl, BaCl2 (summary in Gill et al. 2008)
exp_method_desc: Treated with sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl), HCl, BaCl2 to precipitate BaSO4. Modified Burdett et al. (1987) method, as summarized in Gill et al. (2008).

exp_method_translation: NiS fire assay chemical separation. (CMIBS method)
exp_method_desc: NiS fire assay chemical separation (originally a CMIBS method)
Oriented mount for XRD
Partial digestion, no details provided (originally a NGDB method)

exp_method_translation: PbO fire assay chemical separation (CMIBS method)
exp_method_desc: PbO fire assay chemical separation (originally a CMIBS method)
Mounted in plastic/resin, ground and polished (typically to 0.05 µm) for reflected-light petrography and vitrinite/bitumen reflectance see e.g. ASTM D2797/D7708.

Radiochemical separation (originally a CMIBS method)
Random/unoriented powder mount for XRD

Roasted at 380degC
Roasted at 850degC

Soxhlet type extraction with dichloromethane
Dichloromethane-extraction procedure (modified from Durand and Nicaise 1980) followed by HF-HCl treatment
Dichloromethane-extraction procedure, followed by HCl, HF and HCl, and HNO3 digests

exp_method_desc: Soxhlet type extraction with trichloromethane (aka chloroform)

1M sodium acetate solution buffered with acetic acid to pH 4.5 for 24 hours
1M sodium acetate solution buffered with acetic acid to pH 4.5 for 48 hours, at room temperature
1.0 (+/-0.1) g of powdered sample to 60 mL solution ratio shaken in 1 M Na-­acetate (pH=4), 1 M MgCl2(pH=8), and DI water (mod of Anderson and Delaney 2000, see Beaty 2025)
0.3 M sodium-citrate, 1 M sodium bicarbonate, and 0.14 M sodium dithionite (pH = 7.6) for 8h
2 hrs with 50g/L sodium dithionite solution buffered with 0.35M acetic acid and 0.2M sodium citrate to pH4.8
Citrate-buffered (pH 4.8) sodium dithionite solution, 6hr (modified step in Poulton and Canfield 2005 sequential extraction)

6hrs with 0.2M ammonium oxalate and 0.17M oxalic acid solution buffered with ammonium hydroxide to pH 3.2

1 M HCl 16h

exp_method_desc: Fe-py extracted using 16 M HNO3 (after extraction of oxides/clay via HCl, aluminosilicate via HF), anion exchange chromatography with AGMP-1 resin, sample-standard bracketing. Procedure as in Arnold et al. 2004.

Sinter, unspecified (originally a CMIBS method)
Total digestion, no details provided. (originally a NGDB method)
